_doc: Named card-pool predicates used by outcome.filter in booster-contents JSON. Composition keywords: all (AND), any (OR), not (NOT). Leaves match Scryfall fields. See lib/booster-filters.ts for the full grammar.
_doc_basic_land: Matches both 'Basic Land — X' and 'Basic Snow Land — X'. The engine also uses this filter at the candidate stage to *exclude* basic lands from non-land slots so a common slot doesn't accidentally pull a Plains.
_doc_chase_exclusion: The broad frame treatments (showcase / extended_art / borderless) describe the FRAME only. They deliberately EXCLUDE the dedicated ultra-chase FINISHES (raised-foil anime, serialized, halo/textured/gilded/etc.) — those carry their own promo_types AND have their own dedicated, low-rate slots in the recipes. Without this exclusion a card like Bloomburrow's raised-foil Lumra ($600+) is BOTH showcase AND borderless, so the ordinary 'Showcase Woodland' slot would pull it at full rate and massively inflate pack value. Keep the exclusion list in sync with the dedicated finish filters below.
_doc_borderless_edp_dual: Borderless Elder Dragon, Planeswalker, or non-basic Land — the SOS Collector's chase 'Booster Fun' subtype. Match conditions: border_color is borderless AND the type_line contains (Elder + Dragon) OR Planeswalker OR (Land but not Basic). Subtypes are matched individually so 'Legendary Creature — Dragon Elder' and 'Legendary Creature — Elder Dragon' both qualify.
_doc_double_faced: Transforming / modal double-faced / meld cards — used by the dedicated DFC slot in Innistrad-era draft boosters (ISD, DKA, SOI, EMN).
_doc_special_foils: Specific premium foil finishes Scryfall records in card.promo_types (verified values, lowercase, no separators). Recipes can target these exactly instead of approximating them as plain foil. Add more as new sets introduce them.
_doc_retro_frame: Old-border (1997 frame) retro treatment, e.g. The Brothers' War retro artifacts, Dominaria Remastered retro frame. Matches card.frame.
_doc_full_art_land: Full-art LANDS only (basic or nonbasic). Use this in basic-land slots instead of bare full_art, which would also match full-art creatures/scenes and leak non-lands into the land slot.
_doc_marvel: Marvel Universes Beyond treatments. 'scene_card' matches SPM's multi-card mural scenes — Scryfall tags SPM's promo_types:'poster'. WARNING: do NOT use scene_card for MSH — Scryfall only stamped 'poster' on 3 of MSH's 453 prints (all classic-comic chase mythics), so it silently narrows to the 3 most expensive cards in the set. MSH's Booster Fun sheets are instead identified by collector-number block (verified against the released pool: each block's rarity census matches the Wizards article exactly): panel 297-313 (11R/6M, frame showcase), scene 314-351 (3C/12U/8R/15M, frame inverted, 5 modal_dfc = the double-sided dinner scene), logo 352-379 (15R/13M, frame inverted, 5 modal_dfc), borderless dual lands 380-384 (5R), Gauntlet Mind Stone 386, classic comic 387-401 (15 cards, 4 modal_dfc; Scryfall lists 4 as rare but the article says all mythic, so msh_classic_comic carries no rarity leaf), extended art 402-429 (24R/4M — excludes the 431/432 bundle promos the bare 'extended_art' filter would leak in). Each msh_* filter pairs the CN range with the block's frame_effects so predicateIsAltArtIntent sees a treatment field and skips the implicit regular_print baseline. 'full_art_basic' = the full-art basic-land cycles (SPM spiderweb, MSH city calm/chaos) excluding borderless variants; 'non_full_art_basic' is its complement for the regular-basic share of land slots. 'msh_source_material' selects the MSH-era Source Material sheet: Marvel Universe (MAR) collector numbers 41-100, released alongside MSH — numbers 1-40 are the SPM-era sheet. 'borderless_land' = borderless-frame lands for the wildcard/foil borderless-land outcomes, with the standard ultra-finish exclusion.
_doc_regular_print: Baseline 'looks like the regular printing of this card' filter. The engine applies this implicitly to any outcome whose explicit filter doesn't already declare alt-art intent — so default draft commons come out in their normal art instead of randomly grabbing extended-art / showcase variants. Excludes every Scryfall treatment that signals an alt-art printing: frame_effects (showcase, extendedart, inverted, borderless, companion, shatteredglass, etched, fullart), borderless border_color, and the common alt-art promo_types.
_doc_spg_sheets: Per-set Special Guests sheets within the global SPG set, mapped by collector-number range (each sheet released alongside its parent set — ranges verified against per-print release dates 2026-06). Without these, an SPG outcome could leak another set’s guests; the old special_guest promo filter matched nothing (SPG prints don’t carry promo_types:specialguests).
_doc_source_material: UB source-material sheets. Scryfall introduced promo_types:sourcematerial with TMT (2026); earlier UB sets file source material elsewhere (SPM/MSH -> the MAR set; TLA -> its boosterfun-promo commons).
_doc_field_notes: SOS Field Notes — Scryfall tags these prints frame_effects:showcase (no dedicated fieldnotes effect exists). Filter renamed in meaning, kept in name for recipe stability.
_doc_spellcraft_land: SOS Spellcraft lands — nonbasic lands with the inverted frame effect (no dedicated spellcraft effect exists on Scryfall).
_doc_surge_pixel_land: TMT pixel-art surge-foil basic lands. The land-mentioning shape matters: a bare surge_foil filter in a non-land slot gets the implicit basic-land exclusion applied and can never match these.
_doc_dmu_legends_retold: DMU Legends Retold sheet by collector range — the prints do not carry finishes:etched on Scryfall (the foil-etched variants are separate non-pool prints), so the old etched_foil filter matched nothing.
_doc_variation: Variation prints — Scryfall card.variation=true marks alternate printings of cards already in the set (DSK Lurking Evil, DMU foil-etched Legends Retold variants, J25 anime alt-arts). The bake keeps them; regular_print excludes them from base outcomes; variation_print targets them.
_doc_cosmic_foil: MSH cosmic-foil headliner (The Mind Stone #385) — a unique textless premium finish, promo_types:cosmicfoil. Distinct from galaxy_foil.
_doc_headliner: Scryfall promo_types:headliner — the ~500-copy 'headliner' chase class introduced with HOB's gleaming gold Smaug the Magnificent (#249). Also added to regular_print's and the frame-treatment filters' exclusion lists (with 'boxtopper') so a headliner can never roll as an ordinary mythic — same failure class as the raised-foil Lumra note in _doc_chase_exclusion.
_doc_hob: The Hobbit (HOB, 2026-08-14) + its eternal-legal companion The Hobbit Commander (HOC). Collector-number blocks derived from the Wizards collecting article's image filenames (0202_MTGHOB_ScnBstr, 0217/0226/0229/0237_MTGHOB_Hoard, 0242/0247_MTGHOB_BkCover, 0249_MTGHOB_Headline, 0290/0312_MTGHOB_ExtRM, 0194-0198_MTGHOB_JrnyLand, 0313-0316_MTGHOB_HbbtLand) and the article's sheet censuses, which tile exactly: main set + 5 common duals 1-188, default-frame basics 189-193, journey basics 194-198, scene 199-213 (2C/4U/9R), Dragon hoard 214-238 (6U/11R/8M), book cover 239-248 (4R/6M), gold Smaug headliner 249, surge-foil hoard 250-274, surge-foil book cover 275-284, extended art 285-312 (26R/2M), seasonal Hobbit lands 313-320 (Bundle/Prerelease only — NOT in boosters, excluded from every recipe outcome). Cross-checked against the 127 prints Scryfall had indexed 2026-07-23: #207/#228/#248/#264/#284/#308 each land in the predicted block; #249 carries promo_types:headliner; #266/#270/#271/#283 carry surgefoil. HOC blocks (61 prints indexed): Scene Box scenes 1-12 (all rare), classic artist 13-52 (40 mythic LTR reprints, non-foil sheet), classic artist surge 53-92 (same 40, surge sheet — #76/#78/#84 confirm), Dwarvish language 93-97 (5 mythic, Scryfall lang:'dw' — the 'dwarvish' filter's lang leaf also opts the client pool into keeping dw prints via collectRecipeLanguages). PREVIEW-ERA CAVEAT (the MSH lesson): treatment tagging is inconsistent mid-preview (e.g. only 4 of 40 classic artists carry borderless yet, scene-box prints carry no treatment tags), so these filters are CN-range-first and avoid frame/promo pairings that could either match nothing or over-concentrate onto the few chase prints tagged so far. Pure CN filters don't declare alt-art intent, so the implicit regular_print pass applies — today the untagged prints survive it, and when Scryfall finishes tagging, the pass would empty the block and the engine's keep-unfiltered guard takes over. Both timelines work. AUDIT after release (~2026-08-14, when scripts/build-set-cards.mjs re-bakes hob/hoc/thob): re-run the rarity census per block against the released pool exactly like MSH's AUDIT 2026-07 #2, and confirm the HOC extended-art mythics' CN block (not yet indexed — hob.json's outcome uses the frame-based extended_art filter scoped to HOC until then).
